messages.content: Always use code blocks with the appropriate language tags. If asked for a table always format it using Markdown syntax.
OscPathsInfo.instance?.linkingState != null what does the '?' here measn (note: its csharp)

In C#, the `?.` operator is called the "null-conditional operator". It is used to check for null before accessing a member of an object. If the object on the left side of `?.` is `null`, the expression returns `null` and does not attempt to access the member on the right side.

So, in the expression `OscPathsInfo.instance?.linkingState != null`, if `OscPathsInfo.instance` is `null`, the entire expression will evaluate to `false` (since `null != null` is `false`), without throwing a `NullReferenceException`.
